LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO MARITIME NATIONAL PARK ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S VOLUNTARY DISMISSAL RESULTS IN NO PREVAILING PARTY FOR FEE PURPOSES. CC SECTION 1717(B)(2). REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN BAD FAITH AND AWARDING SANCTIONS UNDER CCP SECTION 128.7 WOULD BE INCONSISTENT WITH THE STATUTE'S SAFE HARBOR PROVISIONS. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER & WILL ALSO SUBMIT A PROPOSED JUDGMENT FOR COSTS. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice And Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Aug-31-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF ANDY KILDAY EVENT PRODUCTION SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS.

APPEARANCES: Dominick R. Libonati, Esq. 650/347-5400 for pltf & moving party; Michael Isaku Begert, Esq. 393-2693 & Diane Barker, Esq. 393-2232 of Bingham McCutchen LLP for deft & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANTED IN PART. COURT STRIKES FEES FOR TRAVEL, MEALS, TAXIS AND DISBURSEMENTS. MOTION DENIED AS TO REMAINDER. (Court has discretion to award messenger fee costs and mediation costs, and does so.) Defendant to submit proposed form of order & will also submit a proposed judgment for costs. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion For Attorneys' Fees
Off Calendar Aug-31-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO MARITIME NATIONAL PARK ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES.

APPEARANCES: Dominick R. Libonati, Esq. 650/347-5400 for pltf & opposing party; Michael Isaku Begert, Esq. 393-2693 & Diane Barker, Esq. 393-2232 of Bingham McCutchen LLP for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S VOLUNTARY DISMISSAL RESULTS IN NO PREVAILING PARTY FOR FEE PURPOSES. CC SECTION 1717(b)(2). REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN BAD FAITH AND AWARDING SANCTIONS UNDER CCP SECTION 128.7 WOULD BE INCONSISTENT WITH THE STATUTE'S SAFE HARBOR PROVISIONS. Defendant to submit proposed form of order & will also submit a proposed judgment for costs. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
